# SEO Destroyer Pro

> Satirical web app that simulates destroying a website's SEO

## Overview

SEO Destroyer Pro v6.6.6 is a parody React application with a retro terminal/hacker aesthetic. Users enter a URL, watch a fake "hacking" terminal sequence, and receive absurd SEO destruction metrics and recommendations. Entirely client-side with no real analysis performed.

## Tech Stack

- React 18 + TypeScript 5.5 (strict mode)
- Vite 5.4 (build and dev server)
- Tailwind CSS 3.4 (minimal usage, mostly inline styles)
- ESLint 9 (flat config with typescript-eslint)

## Project Structure

- src/App.tsx: All application components, data, and logic (~268 lines)
- src/main.tsx: React DOM entry point
- src/index.css: Tailwind CSS imports
- index.html: HTML shell
- vite.config.ts: Vite configuration with React plugin

## Development

Install: npm install
Dev server: npm run dev (localhost:5173)
Build: npm run build (outputs to dist/)
Lint: npm run lint
Type check: npm run typecheck

## Architecture

Single-component application. App.tsx contains:
- Constant data arrays: METRICS (12 items), TERMINAL_LINES (14 items), RECOMMENDATIONS (10 items)
- ScoreGauge component: Animated percentage display
- App component: Phase-based UI (idle/scanning/results) with React hooks
- glitchText utility: Character substitution for glitch effect

No routing, no state management library, no backend, no tests.

## Key Design Decisions

- Monolithic single-file architecture (intentionally simple)
- Inline styles over CSS classes (terminal aesthetic requires computed values)
- All data hardcoded (no API calls by design)
- TypeScript strict mode enforced
- Phase-based state machine for UI flow
